---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

content.text:
These results are dramatic. Here's the summary:

### FPGA Benchmark: 2026-03-19 (baseline) vs 2026-03-20 (post-optimization)

**Every single function got faster.** The changes between these runs include the 4x2 tiled GEMM v4 kernel and GEMM consolidation (commits `5f37b680` and `0dc1c34d`).

**Biggest winners (>80% reduction):**
| Category | Typical Improvement |
|---|---|
| Activation (s8/s16/f16/f32) | **83-97%** faster |
| Elementwise ops (add/mul/sub) | **73-87%** faster |
| Convolution HWC (s8 sym/asym) | **67-71%** faster |
| Conv 1x1 | **62-70%** faster |
| Conv depthwise | **70-75%** faster |
| Conv transposed | **67-80%** faster |
| Fully connected | **43-68%** faster |
| Sigmoid/tanh/silu/swish | **83-85%** faster |
| Concat/split/slice | **70-76%** faster |

**Notable highlights:**
- `activate_s16_hp`: 1,658,989 → 48,521 cycles (**97% reduction**)
- `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any`: 16,855,279 → 5,455,029 (**67.6% reduction**)
- `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_fast`: 7,719,295 → 2,281,305 (**70.4% reduction**)
- `sigmoid_s8`: 993,363 → 145,395 (**85.3% reduction**)

**Minimal/no change:**
- `fc_per_ch_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias`: only -0.3% (already optimized differently)
- `strided_slice_s8`: ~0% (not affected by GEMM changes)

The FPGA board is currently unreachable (`sw-boards.andestech.com:1116`), so I can't run a fresh benchmark right now. These are the most recent results available.

# The instruction count difference (2332 vs 316) for the same binary strongly suggests different VLEN.
# VLEN=1024 processes 4x more elements per vector op than VLEN=256.
# relu_s8 on 65535 elements:
#   VLEN=256 → 32 bytes/op → ~2048 iterations → ~2332 inst makes sense
#   VLEN=1024 → 128 bytes/op → ~512 iterations → ~316 inst makes sense
# Ratio: 2332/316 ≈ 7.4x ≈ not exactly 4x, but close with loop overhead

# Let's check the wrapper scripts used
cat /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv/test_sh_20260319114324/log/relu_s8.log | grep -i "wrapper\|sim-wrapper\|gdb-ice" | head -5
echo "==="

# ENA_RUN_TWICE should print two cycle counts. The fact we see one suggests the first (cold) is suppressed

# More importantly: the 03-19 baseline was on sqa-boards:1115 which is likely a VLEN=256 board
# while 03-20 used sw-boards:1116 which is the VLEN=1024 board we configured.

if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ ENA_HVM ]]; then
        cd ${LIB_ROOT}

        AR="${CC%-*}-ar"
        AR_FLAG="crD"
        libhvm_branch="ast-v5_4_0-release"
        libnhvm_repo_dir="${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project"
        libhvm_name="libhvm"
        libhvm_build_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm.log"
        libhvm_build_error_log="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/build_libhvm_error.log"

        # clone the libhvm repo and switch to the proper branch
        echo "**********************************************"
        echo "Clone and update libhvm ..."
        git submodule update --init --recursive --remote
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        git reset --hard ${libhvm_branch}

        # disable the HVM bank awared
        sed -i 's/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t1/#define CONFIG_HVM_BANK_AWARED\t\t0/' ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm/src/common/nds_hvm_config.h

        # build the libhvm
        echo "Start building libhvm ..."
        cp ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}/Makefile_libhvm ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        cd ${libnhvm_repo_dir}
        make clean -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*}
        make -f Makefile_libhvm COMPILER=${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX%%-*} EXTRA_CFLAGS="-mno-16-bit" V=1 > ${libhvm_build_log} 2> ${libhvm_build_error_log}
        mv ${libnhvm_repo_dir}/libhvm.a ${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}
        echo "Building libhvm done ..."
        echo "**********************************************"

        cd ${LIB_ROOT}  # go back to the repo root
    fi

    echo "**********************************************"
    echo " Building Andes NN Library ..."
    echo "**********************************************"
    echo
    make clean -f ${LIB_ROOT}/Makefile_lib.mak BUILD_DIR="${BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH}"

    mkdir -p pdf
    cp latex/refman.pdf pdf/libnn_user_manual.pdf

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
#
# This script is used to verify libnn accuracy, code coverage and measure
# performance.
#
# Input:
#   $1: Source code path (without / tailed)
#       This is a dummy argument, just for making the API be the same as test.sh
#       of libdsp.
#   $2: Compiler name (with prefix)
#       Set it to the compiler name without full path, e.g., nds32le-elf-gcc,
#       riscv32-elf-clang, riscv64-linux-gcc, etc. This script will try to find
#       the full path of this specified compiler from the PATH environment
#       variable; therefore, please export the proper toolcahin path into PATH
#       environment variable before using this script.
#   $3: Simulator name
#       In DAILY test, please give the full path of simulator.
#   $4: Simulation target
#       It could be GDB, SID or BOARD.
#   $5: BS3 / DAILY
#       BS3:   Verify the accuracy with the built-in multi-lib. (note the sat
#              extension and code coverage won't be verified here)
#       DAILY: Use the user specified compiler and compilation options to build
#              the library, and this built library will be used in the accuracy
#              tests later. Not only the accuracy but also the code coverage
#              will be verified in this mode.
#   $6: Extra flags for selecting multi-lib or the algorithm to be built as library
#       As the 5th argument is set to BS3, these flags are used to select the
#       built-in multi-lib. For example, if "-mext-dsp" is contained in this
#       argument, the DSP version library will be used for linking. By default,
#       the plain C version library will be used for linking.
#       As the 5th argument is set to DAILY, these flags will be used to select
#       the proper algorithm and build them into library. For example, if
#       "-mext-dsp" is contained in this argument, the DSP algorithm will be
#       used as building the library. By default, the plain C algorithm will be
#       used to build the library.
#   $7: Specified linker
#       Use the specified linker instead of the default one when building the
#       executable. If this argument is left to be empty, the default linker
#       will be used. (e.g., ld, lld, etc.)
#
# Example:
#   mkdir build_dir && cd build_dir
#
#   Ex-1: use default linker then test plain C algorithm with V3 GDB run
#   ../test.sh "" nds32le-elf-gcc nds32le-elf-run GDB BS3
#
#   Ex-2: use default linker then test DSP algorithm with RV32 SID
#   ../test.sh "" riscv32-elf-gcc riscv-sim-wrapper SID BS3 "-mext-dsp"

#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="off"

# setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
HVM_PMA="off"

# setup the DDR latency (it could be "on" or "off")
DDR_LATENCY="on"

# enable the non-blocking load (it could be "on" or "off")
NON_BLOCKING_LOAD="on"

# setup the D$ write-around threshold (it could be 0~3)
DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="off"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD $CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

#!/bin/bash
set -e

LIB_PATH=$1    # path to libnn_v.a
LABEL=$2       # "baseline" or "optimized"
OUT_DIR="/tmp/bench_${LABEL}"

export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116

LIBNN=/home/nick/work/libnn
SYSROOT=/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/riscv64-elf

# Install the library
cp "$LIB_PATH" "$SYSROOT/lib/libnn_v.a"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR/adx" "$OUT_DIR/log"

# Tests: 4 GEMM-affected + 2 controls
TESTS="conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any_dilated conv_1xn_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any conv_HWC_s8_s8_s4_asym_bias_any relu_s8 sigmoid_s8"

for t in $TESTS; do
    echo "=== Compiling $t ==="
    riscv64-elf-gcc -O3 -Wall -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections \
      -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP \
      -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1 \
      -DGETENV_TESTDIR=$LIBNN/Examples/unit_func \
      -I$SYSROOT/include \
      -I$LIBNN/Examples/unit_func/include \
      -o "$OUT_DIR/adx/t_${t}.adx" \
      "$LIBNN/Examples/unit_func/t_${t}.c" \
      -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 \
      -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh \
      -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS -lm 2>&1
    
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "COMPILE FAILED: $t"
        continue
    fi
done

# Reset board for first test
rm -f "$LIBNN/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"

for t in $TESTS; do
    echo ""
    echo "=== Running $t on FPGA ($LABEL) ==="
    if [ ! -f "$OUT_DIR/adx/t_${t}.adx" ]; then
        echo "SKIPPED (no binary)"
        continue
    fi
    
    cd "$LIBNN/build_perf_ax45mpv"
    "$LIBNN/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board" "$OUT_DIR/adx/t_${t}.adx" 2>&1 | tee "$OUT_DIR/log/${t}.log"
    
    # Extract cycle count
    cycles=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' "$OUT_DIR/log/${t}.log" | tail -1)
    echo ">>> $t: ${cycles:-FAILED} cycles"
done

echo ""
echo "=== $LABEL SUMMARY ==="
for t in $TESTS; do
    cycles=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' "$OUT_DIR/log/${t}.log" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
    echo "$t: ${cycles:-N/A}"
done
SCRIPT
chmod +x /tmp/run_focused_bench.sh
echo "Ready"

echo ""
echo "=== GEMM-affected tests (conv_HWC using nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset) ==="

for t in conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any conv_1xn_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any_dilated; do
    base=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $BASE/${t}.log | tail -1)
    opt=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $OPT/${t}.log | tail -1)
    if [ -n "$base" ] && [ -n "$opt" ] && [ "$base" -ne 0 ]; then
        speedup=$(echo "scale=2; $base / $opt" | bc)
        pct=$(echo "scale=1; ($base - $opt) * 100 / $base" | bc)
        printf "%-45s %12s %12s %6sx\n" "$t" "$base" "$opt" "$speedup"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Convolution tests (not directly using modified GEMM kernel) ==="

for t in conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast conv_HWC_s8_s16_s8_sym_bias_fast; do
    base=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $BASE/${t}.log | tail -1)
    opt=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $OPT/${t}.log | tail -1)
    if [ -n "$base" ] && [ -n "$opt" ] && [ "$base" -ne 0 ]; then
        speedup=$(echo "scale=2; $base / $opt" | bc)
        printf "%-45s %12s %12s %6sx\n" "$t" "$base" "$opt" "$speedup"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Control tests (no GEMM changes expected) ==="

for t in relu_s8 sigmoid_s8; do
    base=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $BASE/${t}.log | tail -1)
    opt=$(grep -oP 'The cycle count is \K[0-9]+' $OPT/${t}.log | tail -1)
    if [ -n "$base" ] && [ -n "$opt" ] && [ "$base" -ne 0 ]; then
        speedup=$(echo "scale=2; $base / $opt" | bc)
        printf "%-45s %12s %12s %6sx\n" "$t" "$base" "$opt" "$speedup"
    fi
done

content.text:
Now we have a proper apples-to-apples comparison on the same FPGA board.

### Results: GEMM v4 Optimization on FPGA (sw-boards:1116, VLEN=1024)

**Affected:**
| Test | Baseline | Optimized | Speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any` | 5,453,441 | 1,775,759 | **3.07x** |
| `conv_1xn_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any` | 38,217 | 27,376 | **1.39x** |

**Unaffected (as expected):**
| Test | Baseline | Optimized | Speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any_dilated` | 1,822,840 | 1,818,852 | 1.00x |
| `conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any` | 1,116,801 | 1,116,911 | 1.00x |
| `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_sym_bias_fast` | 2,292,485 | 2,292,503 | 1.00x |
| `relu_s8` / `sigmoid_s8` | unchanged | unchanged | 1.00x |

**Key takeaways:**
- The GEMM v4 kernel gives a real **3.07x speedup** on `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any` — matches the 3.09x from QEMU prediction
- `conv_1xn` benefits partially (1.39x) — it uses the same GEMM kernel but has smaller matrix sizes so less tiling benefit
- The dilated variant shows no change — it likely takes a different code path that doesn't call the modified kernel
- All non-GEMM functions are completely unchanged (noise within ±0.3%), confirming the optimization is surgical
